concept: Tool / Implement / To Use as Instrument
description: An implement used to carry out a particular function; to use something as an instrument. 'Shoru' names what extends human capability—the shaped object that amplifies intention into action.
sound_symbolism: The word shapes purpose. 'Sho' /ʃo/ focuses intent with directed energy. Then 'ru' rolls into action, tool becoming extension of hand.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo shoru' (the tool). As verb: 'mi shoru' (I tool/use as tool). Related to 'shoruka' (workshop)—where tools live and work happens.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Appropriate technology—tools scaled to human needs, repairable, understandable.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Tools as craft—each tool learned through apprenticeship, becoming extension of maker's skill.
